message:
fix(fr-command): EVAL error wording matches upstream [bd-frankenredis-fo1s]

Two Lua-script error wording divergences from upstream Redis 7.2:

- redis.call / redis.pcall on an unknown command: fr previously
  bubbled up the literal "ERR unknown command 'NOPE', with args
  beginning with: " from dispatch_argv. Upstream
  script_lua.c::luaRedisGenericCommand replaces this surface with
  "Unknown Redis command called from script" before returning
  it. Detect the unknown-command prefix in lua_eval::redis_call
  and rewrite. (Upstream additionally appends script SHA1 +
  source location for the redis.call (raise-error) variant; that
  context is left as a follow-up since it requires propagating
  the script SHA1 + line through the LuaState error path.)

- Lua compile errors: fr's tokenizer emitted "unexpected
  character 'X'" while upstream Lua emits "unexpected symbol
  near 'X'". Updated both tokenizer call sites. The fr-command
  eval_script_error_reply wrapper now also detects compile
  errors and prefixes them with "Error compiling script (new
  function): user_script:1: " (matching upstream
  script_lua.c::luaCreateFunction error wrapping).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
